FULL CONTENT:
![](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/industries/2022/public-safety/emergency-management/assets/disaster/industry-emergency-management-disaster-res-banner-small-background.jpg)

# Damage Assessment

Collect, manage, and report damage assessments

![A damage assessment map with a dark section down the middle, and an aerial view of tents with bags of resources](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/industries/2022/public-safety/emergency-management/assets/damage-assessment/industry-emergency-management-damage-assess-banner-small-foreground.png)

Understanding the impact of a disaster is key for accurately deploying resources and helping a community recover. Esri's damage assessment solution is aligned with Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) programs. The solution supports damage report collection, assigning initial damage assessments, monitoring the impact of a disaster, briefing stakeholders, and sharing information.

### ArcGIS solutions for damage assessment

Understand the severity and magnitude of any incident—rapidly collect, manage, and report disaster damage.

![smart-communities-64](/en-us/industries/emergency-management/solutions/damage-assessment/_jcr_content/par/grid_container/gc-par/tabs/item_1599254919909/image.coreimg.svg/1746135056314/smart-communities-64.svg "smart-communities-64")

## Engage the community and solicit input

During an emergency, residents should be able to find the incident impact information and learn how recovery activities affect them. The damage assessment site created with ArcGIS Hub can be used to share damage assessment information and collect damage reports from the community to aid and expedite the overall damage assessment process.

During an emergency, residents should be able to find the incident impact information and learn how recovery activities affect them. The damage assessment site created with ArcGIS Hub can be used to share damage assessment information and collect damage reports from the community to aid and expedite the overall damage assessment process.

![A desktop computer displaying a damage assessment photo viewer and map with the locations of the photos](https://esri.scene7.com/is/image/esri/industry-emergency-management-damage-assess-tab-1?qlt=82&ts=1746135056520&fmt=png-alpha&dpr=off)
![](https://esri.scene7.com/is/content/esri/damage-assessment-64?ts=1746135056690&dpr=off)

## Deploy field teams and collect data

ArcGIS Hub provides damage assessment resources to local officials, public safety staff, and volunteers so that they can quickly assess damage severity. The preliminary damage assessment helps staff rapidly capture damage reports, while the more detailed Individual Assistance and Public Assistance surveys collect information required by FEMA.

ArcGIS Hub provides damage assessment resources to local officials, public safety staff, and volunteers so that they can quickly assess damage severity. The preliminary damage assessment helps staff rapidly capture damage reports, while the more detailed Individual Assistance and Public Assistance surveys collect information required by FEMA.

![A desktop computer displaying a damage assessment web browser with a QR code and a second browse open with an individual assistance survey](https://esri.scene7.com/is/image/esri/industry-emergency-management-damage-assess-tab-2?qlt=82&ts=1746135056913&fmt=png-alpha&dpr=off)
![report-64](/en-us/industries/emergency-management/solutions/damage-assessment/_jcr_content/par/grid_container/gc-par/tabs/item_1599255656419/image.coreimg.svg/1746135057094/report-64.svg "report-64")

## Monitor progress and reporting

As public damage reports are submitted, the Damage Assessment Operations app can be used by emergency management staff to manage, monitor, and administer damage assessments. The information submitted can then act as a starting point for initial damage assessments conducted by government officials.

As public damage reports are submitted, the Damage Assessment Operations app can be used by emergency management staff to manage, monitor, and administer damage assessments. The information submitted can then act as a starting point for initial damage assessments conducted by government officials.

![A desktop computer displaying a damage assessment operations dashboard](https://esri.scene7.com/is/image/esri/industry-emergency-management-damage-assess-tab-3?qlt=82&ts=1746135057304&fmt=png-alpha&dpr=off)
![](/en-us/industries/emergency-management/solutions/damage-assessment/_jcr_content/par/grid_container/gc-par/tabs/item_1644278918924/image_copy.coreimg.svg/1746135057535/shared-data-64.svg)

## Manage and share information

Damage information is a valuable resource used by many agencies to make critical decisions related to recovery resources. The Damage Assessment solution includes a series of tools that help you manage incident information. Create provision field users, share information with partner agencies, and export or report incident data to state and federal partners.

Damage information is a valuable resource used by many agencies to make critical decisions related to recovery resources. The Damage Assessment solution includes a series of tools that help you manage incident information. Create provision field users, share information with partner agencies, and export or report incident data to state and federal partners.

![A desktop computer displaying a damage assessment operations web browser with a map showing effected home locations](https://esri.scene7.com/is/image/esri/industry-emergency-management-damage-assess-tab-4?qlt=82&ts=1746135057738&fmt=png-alpha&dpr=off)

Oklahoma Department of Emergency Management (OEM) takes an innovative approach to damage, using ArcGIS Survey123.

## City of Key West mobilized field teams

With communications and power down after Hurricane Irma, the City of Key West mobilized teams for damage assessment to expedite disaster funding.

## FEMA utilizes GIS and AI for damage assessments

Automated preliminary response estimates—using artificial intelligence (AI) in ArcGIS—reduce the assessment and rescue work time from days to hours.

![An aerial view of homes and trees flooded with muddy water](/content/dam/esrisites/en-us/industries/2022/public-safety/emergency-management/assets/damage-assessment/industry-emergency-management-damage-assess-thumb-1.jpg)

## ArcGIS Solutions and products for damage assessment

Explore the ArcGIS Solution Damage Assessment and other products to enhance damage assessments for emergency and disaster management.

### ArcGIS Survey123

ArcGIS Survey123 is a simple, intuitive formcentric data-gathering solution that works via the web or mobile devices, even when disconnected.

### ArcGIS Field Maps

ArcGIS Field Maps is an all-in-one app that uses data-driven maps to help mobile workers streamline critical workflows.

### ArcGIS Dashboards

ArcGIS Dashboards helps make decisions, visualize trends, monitor status in real time, and inform mission-critical decisions.

### ArcGIS StoryMaps

Combine your narrative with interactive maps and other multimedia content to communicate and engage stakeholders around resilience.

### Damage Assessment

Conduct initial damage assessments after a natural disaster or catastrophic incident.

SUMMARY:
FEMA is developing the capability to understand the full extent of disaster impacts within 72 hours post-incident using Geospatial Damage Assessments.

enriched_points.expanded_content: GIS enables rapid damage assessment post-disaster, allowing for accurate information collection to expedite recovery efforts and inform decision-making.
Utilizing GIS for resource deployment ensures optimized allocation of aid, personnel, and equipment to areas most affected, enhancing response efficiency.
GIS supports the development of long-term recovery plans by analyzing spatial data to identify needs, risks, and opportunities for community rebuilding.
GIS fosters community resilience by visualizing vulnerabilities and strengths, enabling stakeholders to implement strategies that enhance preparedness and recovery.
Notable case studies, such as FEMA's geospatial assessments, demonstrate the effectiveness of GIS in coordinating rapid recovery efforts post-natural disasters.
